briefdescription.para: number of values from 0
number of allocated values
number of values of null probability from 0
maximum probability
complementary probability (> 0. for improper distributions)
mean
variance
number of free parameters
probability mass function
cumulative distribution function
Copy of the probability mass function.

parameterdescription.para: number of values. inb_value can be less than dist.nb_values. In this case, the unnormalized truncated distribution is copied

briefdescription.para: Copy of a Distribution object in the case where the number of allocated values is the same for the 2 Distribution objects.
Initialization of a Distribution object.
Copy of a Distribution object.
Copy of a Distribution object with renormalization.
Constructor of the Distribution class.
Constructor of the Distribution class.
Construction of a Distribution object from an initial Distribution object applying a scaling operation.
Construction of a Distribution object from a FrequencyDistribution object.
Copy constructor of the Distribution class.
Destructor of the Distribution class.
Assignment operator of the Distribution class.
Equality operator of the Distribution class.

simplesect.para: equality or not of the discrete distributions.

briefdescription.para: Writing of the characteristics of a discrete distribution.

parameterdescription.para: flag on the writing of the shape characteristics,

memberdef.argsstring: (std::ostream &os, bool comment_flag, bool cumul_flag, bool nb_value_flag, const FrequencyDistribution *histo=NULL) const

memberdef.name: ascii_print

briefdescription.para: Display of a discrete distribution.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::ostream &os, int nb_dist, const Distribution **dist, double *dist_scale, bool cumul_flag, const FrequencyDistribution *histo=NULL, bool mass_first=false) const
memberdef.name: spreadsheet_print

briefdescription.para: Computation of the number of plotted values (Gnuplot output).
Writing of a discrete distribution at the Gnuplot format.
Writing of a discrete distribution and a frequency distribution at the Gnuplot format.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *path, int nb_dist, const Distribution **dist, double *scale, int *dist_nb_value, int nb_histo, const FrequencyDistribution **histo)

briefdescription.para: Writing of a discrete distribution and the associated survivor function at the Gnuplot format.

memberdef.argsstring: (StatError &error, const char *prefix, int nb_dist, const Distribution **idist, const char *title) const
memberdef.name: plot_write

briefdescription.para: Plot of family of a discrete distributions using Gnuplot:

listitem.para: probability mass functions and cumulative distribution functions,

briefdescription.para: Writing of the probability mass function.
Writing of the cumulative discrete distribution.
Writing of the matching of the cumulative distribution function with the cumulative distribution function of a reference distribution.
Writing of the concentration curve deduced from a discrete distribution.
Computation and writing of the survivor function of a discrete distribution.
Plot of a discrete distribution.
Plot of family of a discrete distributions:

listitem.para: probability mass functions and cumulative distribution functions,

briefdescription.para: Computation of the survival rates from a discrete distribution and writing of the result.
Computation of survival rates from a discrete distribution and writing of the result in a file at the spreadsheet format.
Computation of the survival rates from a discrete distribution and plot of the result using Gnuplot.
Computation of survival rates from a discrete distribution and plot of the result.
Extraction of the probability at the mode of a discrete distribution.
Extraction of the mode of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the mean of a discrete distribution.
Computation of a quantile of a discrete distribution.

parameterdescription.para: value of the cumulative distribution function.

briefdescription.para: Computation of the variance of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the number of possible values from 0.
Computation of the number of values of null probability from 0.
Computation of the coefficient of concentration of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the mean absolute deviation of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the coefficient of skewness of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the excess kurtosis of a discrete distribution: excess kurtosis = coefficient of kurtosis - 3.
Computation of the information quantity of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the sum of squared first-order differences.
Computation of the sum of squared second-order differences.
Computation of the cumulative distribution function of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the survivor function of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the concentration function of a discrete distribution.
Computation of the distance between 2 discrete distributions (1 - overlap).
Ccomputation of the log-probability mass function.
Computation of the log-likelihood of the survival function of a given distribution.
Computation of the chi2 value for a discrete distribution fit (Chi2 goodness of fit test).
Grouping of successive values, computation of the degrees of freedom and the chi2 value (Chi2 goodness of fit test).

memberdef.argsstring: (double weight, penalty_type pen_type, double *penalty, side_effect outside) const
memberdef.name: penalty_computation

briefdescription.para: Computation of the penalty terms in the framework of a penalized likelihood approach.

parameterdescription.para: penalty type (1st order, 2nd order difference or entropy),
management of side effects (zero outside the support or continuation of the distribution).

briefdescription.para: Chi2 goodness of fit test for a discrete distribution.
Convolution of 2 distributions (the convolution can be put in one of the 2 distributions).

parameterdescription.para: number of values of the convolution.

briefdescription.para: Simulation using the cumulative distribution function.
Truncation of a distribution.
Discrete distribution.

detaileddescription.para: Probabilities are represented as an array *mass, with size nb_allocated_value mass[i] is only meaningful for offset <= i < nb_value. For i < offset or i >= nb_value, mass[i] may be either 0 or unspecified. nb_allocated_value has to be greater or equal to nb_value. It may happen that nb_allocated_value > nb_value, particularly when mass[i] is computed for every i <= quantile(0.9999) = nb_value-1, which we do not know in advance, but we allocated nb_allocated_value > nb_value for the sake of safety.
